game_intro: Neuraminidase is the door handle the flu virus uses to escape from your cells. Oseltamivir (Tamiflu) already weakens it — can you build something even better?
boss_flavor: This enzyme has triggered pandemics throughout history. It sits on the viral surface, releasing copies of the virus into your body every second.
plain_english: The flu virus uses this enzyme to copy itself and spread to new cells. Your molecule needs to block it better than Tamiflu.

patient_story.name: Maya
patient_story.condition: Influenza A H3N2 with respiratory distress
patient_story.urgency: Admitted to ICU — Tamiflu resistance detected, oxygen saturation dropping
patient_story.outcome_win: Maya's fever broke overnight. Her parents cried when she asked for breakfast. She goes home in two days.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Viral load continued rising despite Tamiflu. Maya required mechanical ventilation. The lab needs more time.

mutation_pool.name: Efflux Activator
mutation_pool.description: NA activates membrane efflux pumps, expelling small molecules before they can bind.

mutation_pool.name: R292K Resistance
mutation_pool.description: A single point mutation reshapes the active site, reducing oseltamivir-like binding by 1000×.

phase2_taunt: My R292K variant laughs at your Tamiflu analogues. Evolve or fail.
phase3_taunt: Pandemic mode engaged. Every cell in this body is a factory now.

game_intro: SARS-CoV-2 uses this protease to cut viral proteins into working pieces. Nirmatrelvir (Paxlovid) already weakens it — can you design a better molecule?
boss_flavor: This molecular scissors is critical for COVID-19 replication. Without it, the virus cannot assemble itself. Real drug: Paxlovid (approved 2021).
plain_english: The COVID-19 virus uses this molecular scissors to build itself. Your job is to design a molecule that jams those scissors better than Paxlovid.

patient_story.name: Harold
patient_story.condition: Severe COVID-19 pneumonia, immunocompromised post-kidney transplant
patient_story.urgency: Rebound infection after first Paxlovid course — viral replication persisting in lung tissue
patient_story.outcome_win: Harold's CT scan cleared within a week. He called his daughter from the ward. Discharge in four days.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Secondary bacterial pneumonia set in as COVID-19 suppressed his immune response. Harold transferred to high-dependency care.

mutation_pool.name: Overexpression Surge
mutation_pool.description: Viral replication drives Mpro concentration 8× above baseline, overwhelming inhibitor coverage.

mutation_pool.name: Membrane Micro-Shield
mutation_pool.description: Viral remodelling of endosomal membranes reduces inhibitor diffusion to the cleavage site.

phase2_taunt: Paxlovid rebound is real. Your molecules are just speed bumps.
phase3_taunt: I replicate faster than you can design. This patient is mine.

game_intro: HIV Protease assembles viral proteins for HIV. Cut off one resistance pathway and it mutates another. No full seed provided — design your own strategy.
boss_flavor: One of the most mutated drug targets in history. HIV constantly evolves new resistance mechanisms — like a hydra that grows back every time you cut a head.
plain_english: HIV needs this enzyme to mature into an infectious virus. Block it and the virus produces harmless, immature particles that cannot infect new cells.

patient_story.name: Amara
patient_story.condition: HIV-1 with multi-drug resistant protease (D30N + M46I + I84V)
patient_story.urgency: Third-line ART failing — viral load 180,000 copies/mL, CD4 count 42
patient_story.outcome_win: Viral load dropped below 50 copies/mL within 12 weeks. Amara resumed her teaching career. Her daughter was born HIV-negative.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Resistance panel showed no remaining approved options. Amara was enrolled in a compassionate use programme. The fight continues.

mutation_pool.name: D30N Flap Mutation
mutation_pool.description: Mutations in the flap region reduce inhibitor binding affinity by blocking the catalytic dyad.

mutation_pool.name: P-gp Efflux Upregulation
mutation_pool.description: HIV-infected cells upregulate P-glycoprotein, actively pumping protease inhibitors out.

phase2_taunt: D30N, M46I, I84V — every mutation is a door slamming in your face.
phase3_taunt: I have survived every drug class. I am evolution itself.

game_intro: EGFR drives uncontrolled cell growth in lung cancer. Gefitinib targets it, but resistance mutations are common. Can you improve on existing drugs?
boss_flavor: This kinase tells cancer cells to keep dividing, no matter what. It is mutated in roughly 15% of non-small cell lung cancers worldwide.
plain_english: EGFR acts as the gas pedal for cancer cell growth. Your molecule needs to remove the foot from the pedal to stop cancer from spreading.

patient_story.name: David
patient_story.condition: Stage IIIB non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR exon 19 deletion
patient_story.urgency: Gefitinib resistance emerged after 14 months — T790M mutation confirmed on liquid biopsy
patient_story.outcome_win: PET scan showed 70% tumour regression. David watched his son graduate. Maintenance therapy ongoing with good tolerance.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Progressive disease on second-line agents. David enrolled in a clinical trial. His oncologist remains cautiously hopeful.

mutation_pool.name: T790M Gatekeeper
mutation_pool.description: The gatekeeper T790M mutation sterically blocks gefitinib-class inhibitors from accessing the ATP pocket.

mutation_pool.name: EGFR Amplification
mutation_pool.description: Gene amplification drives 40× overexpression, overwhelming stoichiometric inhibition.

phase2_taunt: T790M makes first-generation inhibitors obsolete. What's your next move?
phase3_taunt: Forty copies of my gene. You cannot saturate all of them.

game_intro: BRAF V600E is a drug-resistant mutation in melanoma. The usual drugs no longer work. You need a molecule that specifically targets the mutated form.
boss_flavor: A single amino acid change — V600E — locks this protein permanently in the 'on' position, turning it into a cancer driver. Existing drugs struggle against this mutation.
plain_english: A mutation in BRAF locks the cancer accelerator permanently 'on'. You need a molecule that fits the mutated shape specifically, not the normal shape.

patient_story.name: Priya
patient_story.condition: Metastatic BRAF V600E melanoma with brain lesions, Vemurafenib-resistant
patient_story.urgency: Paradoxical MAPK reactivation under MEK inhibitor combination — lesions growing at 2mm/week
patient_story.outcome_win: Stereotactic radiation plus your compound achieved intracranial response. Priya returned to her architecture practice six months later.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Resistance through BRAF splice variants rendered the inhibitor ineffective. Priya began immunotherapy, searching for another path.

mutation_pool.name: RAS-Independent Dimerisation
mutation_pool.description: V600E BRAF forms RAS-independent dimers that are inherently resistant to ATP-competitive inhibitors.

mutation_pool.name: MDR1 Induction
mutation_pool.description: BRAF activation induces MDR1 expression, increasing drug efflux from tumour cells.

phase2_taunt: Paradoxical MAPK reactivation. Your inhibitor just made me stronger.
phase3_taunt: Splice variants. Copy number gain. I am not a single target — I am a family.

game_intro: SIRT1 is linked to aging and longevity research. This is a frontier target — no approved drug yet. You are working at the very edge of science.
boss_flavor: SIRT1 controls hundreds of genes linked to metabolism, stress response, and aging. Scientists believe activating it may slow the aging process itself.
plain_english: This protein regulates how we age. Designing molecules that interact with it could one day help us live healthier, longer lives — but the science is still being discovered.

patient_story.name: Kenji
patient_story.condition: Accelerated cellular senescence with type 2 diabetes and early Alzheimer's pathology
patient_story.urgency: Enrolled in phase-0 longevity trial — SIRT1 activity 40% below age-matched controls on biopsy
patient_story.outcome_win: Cognitive assessments stabilised at 12 weeks. Kenji's family said he seemed 'sharper'. He finished writing his memoir.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Senescent cell burden continued rising without effective SIRT1 modulation. Kenji's condition progressed as expected. The longevity trial continues.

mutation_pool.name: DBC1 Sequestration
mutation_pool.description: Elevated DBC1 sequesters SIRT1's allosteric activating domain, reducing apparent binding affinity.

mutation_pool.name: Nuclear Export Shield
mutation_pool.description: Stress-induced nuclear export traps SIRT1 in cytoplasmic complexes inaccessible to most activators.

phase2_taunt: DBC1 has me locked in an inhibited state. Modulate the allosteric site if you dare.
phase3_taunt: I have been regulating lifespan for a billion years of evolution. You have been a scientist for what — twenty?

game_intro: CDK2 drives the cell division cycle in multiple cancers. Expert level — no hints, AI Army mode recommended. This is the final boss.
boss_flavor: CDK2 is the master timer of cell division. When hijacked by cancer, cells divide endlessly. It is implicated in breast, colon, and many other cancers.
plain_english: CDK2 is the countdown timer that tells cells when to divide. Cancer removes the countdown, so cells divide forever. Stopping CDK2 restores the timer.

patient_story.name: Sofia
patient_story.condition: Triple-negative breast cancer with CDK2 amplification, relapsed after two lines of chemotherapy
patient_story.urgency: Liver metastases growing rapidly — ECOG performance status declining, limited time before organ failure
patient_story.outcome_win: Complete metabolic response on PET at 8 weeks. Sofia married her partner in the hospital garden. Two years later, she is in clinical remission.
patient_story.outcome_loss: Tumour heterogeneity with CDK2 and CDK4 co-amplification evaded single-target inhibition. Sofia entered palliative care. Her case changed how we design trials.

mutation_pool.name: ABCG2 Efflux Overload
mutation_pool.description: Cancer stem cells upregulate ABCG2, a high-affinity efflux transporter that rapidly clears CDK2 inhibitors.

mutation_pool.name: Cyclin E1 Overcrowding
mutation_pool.description: Cyclin E1 overexpression shifts CDK2 into a conformation that excludes most known inhibitor scaffolds.

phase2_taunt: Cyclin E1 overexpression. Your scaffold cannot reach my active site now.
phase3_taunt: I am the last checkpoint between this patient's cells and immortality. Defeat me if you can, scientist.
